title: Flavor da Casa
description: A production-grade restaurant reservation system built as a multi-module Maven project with Spring Boot. Features Spring Security authentication with JWT-protected REST APIs, a full reservation workflow with approval/denial and automated email notifications, menu management with items, seating, and dining table configuration, and Swagger API documentation, all following strict Clean Architecture principles across separate application modules.
challenge: Building a restaurant management platform that goes beyond basic CRUD, handling a multi-step reservation workflow (request → pending → approve/deny), enforcing authentication for staff operations while keeping the guest-facing reservation form public, sending automated email notifications on status changes, and exposing a separate stateless REST API with JWT bearer token authentication for third-party integrations. All of this needed to maintain strict Clean Architecture boundaries across a multi-module Maven project with shared common libraries, while being developed collaboratively through Jira Sprint cycles.
solution: We architected the system as a multi-module Maven project with clear module separation: a shared common library (common-domain, common-application, common-web, common-infrastructure), a restaurant module (domain, application, infrastructure, web), an authentication module (auth-domain, auth-application, auth-persistence), and two runnable applications, app-web (Thymeleaf UI on port 8080) and app-restaurant-rest-api (stateless JWT API on port 8081). Spring Security handles form-based login for the web UI and enforces @PreAuthorize on staff-only endpoints, while the REST API uses an OAuth2 resource server with RSA key-pair JWT validation. The reservation workflow validates event/seating existence, creates a UUID-tracked reservation in PENDING status, sends a confirmation email, and then allows authenticated staff to approve (with table assignment) or deny, each triggering a status update email to the guest. Swagger/OpenAPI documentation is auto-generated for all API endpoints.

features.title: Multi-Module Clean Architecture
features.desc: Maven multi-module project with shared common libraries. Each module (restaurant, auth, common) has its own Domain, Application, Infrastructure, and Presentation layers with strict dependency inversion.

features.title: Spring Security Authentication
features.desc: Form-based login for the web UI with @PreAuthorize annotations on staff-only endpoints. Public access for guest reservation requests and menu browsing.

features.title: JWT-Protected REST API
features.desc: Separate stateless REST API application (port 8081) secured with OAuth2 Resource Server and RSA key-pair JWT validation. Bearer token authentication for all protected endpoints.

features.title: Reservation Approval Workflow
features.desc: Multi-step flow: guest submits request → PENDING status with UUID confirmation → staff reviews with table assignment → APPROVED or DENIED. Each transition triggers validation and email notification.

features.title: Automated Email Notifications
features.desc: EmailService sends formatted reservation confirmations on request, approval notifications with reservation details, and denial notifications, all with dynamic content such as event name, seating time, and confirmation code.

features.title: Menu & Item Management
features.desc: Full CRUD for menus and menu items with descriptions (e.g., Chef's Tasting Menu with courses: Amuse-Bouche, Bisque, Main). Menus link to events and display on the reservation form.

features.title: Seating & Dining Table System
features.desc: Seating CRUD with table assignments (many-to-many via SeatingTableEntity), capacity tracking, overlapping seating conflict detection, and cascade delete management.

features.title: Reservation List with Filtering
features.desc: Staff dashboard with reservation list filterable by event and status (PENDING / APPROVED / DENIED). Detail view shows full guest info, event, seating, and table assignment controls.

features.title: Swagger / OpenAPI Documentation
features.desc: Auto-generated API documentation via SpringDoc OpenAPI with bearer token authentication scheme configured for all protected endpoints.

features.title: Data Initialization Service
features.desc: InitializeDataServiceImpl seeds the database with default dining tables, events, seatings, menus, and menu items on first startup, enabling immediate demo readiness.

features.title: Validation Framework
features.desc: Custom ValidationService pattern per entity with Result/ValidatedResult wrapper types, field-level error binding to BindingResult, and dedicated validation services for Events, Seatings, Menus, MenuItems, DiningTables, and Reservations.

features.title: Agile with Jira
features.desc: Sprint-managed development with user stories, subtasks, and a Git branching strategy (main → sprint → feature branches) with Pull Request reviews between team members.

gallery.caption: Reservation Dashboard - filterable by event and status, with color-coded badges
Swagger API Documentation - JWT-secured REST endpoints
Spring Security Login - form-based authentication for staff
Admin Dashboard - event listing with management controls
